name: Satchidanandendra Saraswati
dates_notes: Born 5 January 1880; died 5 August 1975; founder of the Adhyātma-Prakāśa-Kāryālaya, Holenarsipur, Hassan district, Karnataka.

lineage_polemical.note: Mūlāvidyā-Nirāsa argues that the doctrine of mūlāvidyā as positive material-cause is post-Śaṅkaran and absent from Śaṅkara's own bhāṣyas. The Vivaraṇa Brahman-as-locus-of-avidyā thesis is therefore rejected as un-Śaṅkaran.

The Bhāmatī jīva-as-locus thesis is likewise rejected as un-Śaṅkaran. For Śaṅkara himself, on Satchidānandendra's reading, avidyā is adhyāsa (superimposition) rather than a substantive material cause requiring a locus.
Twentieth-century intra-Advaita polemic; the Anantakṛṣṇa Śāstrī line defends the post-Śaṅkara scholastic apparatus against the Holenarsipur revisionism.

core_thesis: Satchidānandendra Sarasvatī, founder of the Adhyātma-Prakāśa-Kāryālaya at Holenarsipur, challenges the entire post-Śaṅkara scholastic edifice as a deviation from Śaṅkara's own *bhāṣya*-method. His *Mūlāvidyā-Nirāsa* (1929, Sanskrit) argues that the doctrine of *mūlāvidyā* as a positive material-cause of the world — central to Vivaraṇa Advaita and tacitly accepted by Bhāmatī — is foreign to Śaṅkara's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, *Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Bhāṣya*, and *Upadeśa-Sāhasrī*. For Śaṅkara, on this reading, *avidyā* is *adhyāsa* (superimposition), not a substance whose locus must then be assigned. The argument is destructive: if *mūlāvidyā* were Śaṅkara's own doctrine, the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* prefacing BSB 1.1.1 would be inexplicable. He systematises the recovery as the *Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā* school. The extensive Sanskrit and Kannada output — *Vedānta-Prakriyā-Pratyabhijñā*, *Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā-Bhāskara*, *Māṇḍūkya-Rahasya-Vivṛti* — re-reads the post-Śaṅkara tradition selectively, accepting Sureśvara, Padmapāda, Maṇḍana, Vāchaspati, and Prakāśātman only insofar as they conform to the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* method. The intra-Advaita controversy he initiated remains live.

engaged_works.title: Mulavidya-Nirasa
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit polemical *prakaraṇa* arguing that the doctrine of *mūlāvidyā* — *avidyā* as a positive material cause of the world, requiring a locus (*āśraya*) and an object (*viṣaya*) — is absent from Śaṅkara's own *bhāṣya*-corpus. The argument proceeds passage by passage. Each canonical post-Śaṅkara text invoked in defense of *mūlāvidyā* is matched against the *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, the *Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Bhāṣya*, and the *Upadeśa-Sāhasrī*; in each case, on Satchidānandendra's reading, what Śaṅkara himself says is *adhyāsa*-talk, not *mūlāvidyā*-talk. The destructive moment of the argument is that the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* prefacing BSB 1.1.1 is inexplicable if *avidyā* is a positively existing material cause: superimposition presupposes the cogniser already in place, not a substance from which the cogniser is generated. The work launches the Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā movement and is the principal documentary source for the twentieth-century intra-Advaita controversy on the locus and ontological status of *avidyā*.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Prakriya-Pratyabhijna
engaged_works.summary: A constructive Sanskrit *prakaraṇa* setting out the *prakriyā* (method) of Śaṅkara's *bhāṣya*-corpus as Satchidānandendra reconstructs it, after the destructive work of the *Mūlāvidyā-Nirāsa*. The treatise treats *adhyāsa* as the load-bearing concept, the *mahā-vākyas* as direct *jñāna*-generating instruments rather than secondary supports for *mūlāvidyā*-removal, and *jñāna-niṣṭhā* as the *sādhana*-foreground. The work is the school's positive doctrinal handbook — what *Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā* affirms, not just what it denies — and is the natural complement to the *Mūlāvidyā-Nirāsa* on the negative side. The treatise also fixes the school's reading of the *prasthāna-traya*: Śaṅkara's own *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, *Gītā-Bhāṣya*, and ten principal Upaniṣad-bhāṣyas are taken as the doctrinal frontier, and the *prakaraṇa* literature including the *Upadeśa-Sāhasrī* is read as continuous with the *bhāṣya*-corpus while later Vivaraṇa and Bhāmatī materials are read as deviations.

engaged_works.title: Shuddha-Shankara-Prakriya-Bhaskara
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit illuminator (*bhāskara*) of the *Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā*. The work supplies the school's reading of each contested locus in the post-Śaṅkara tradition — Sureśvara on *avidyā*, Padmapāda on the *jīva-Brahma* superimposition, Maṇḍana on *vidhi*, Vāchaspati on the locus of *avidyā*, Prakāśātman on the *vivaraṇa* of the *Pañca-pādikā* — and indicates in each case which moves are continuous with the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* method and which are doctrinal accretions. The treatise functions as the school's reception-history of post-Śaṅkara Advaita conducted on its own methodological criterion. The treatment of Sureśvara is unusually sympathetic — Sureśvara is read as preserving the *adhyāsa* analysis through the *Naiṣkarmya-Siddhi* — while the treatment of Vāchaspati's *Bhāmatī* and Prakāśātman's *Pañca-pādikā-Vivaraṇa* is correspondingly more critical. The work is one of the school's principal reception-historical reference texts.

engaged_works.title: Mandukya-Rahasya-Vivriti
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on the *Māṇḍūkya Upaniṣad* together with Gauḍapāda's *Kārikās*. The reading is constructed to demonstrate that the *Māṇḍūkya*-Gauḍapāda-Śaṅkara stratum supports the *adhyāsa* analysis of *avidyā* and not the post-Śaṅkara *mūlāvidyā* doctrine. The four-*pāda* analysis of consciousness — *jāgrat*, *svapna*, *suṣupti*, *turīya* — is treated as the analytic structure within which *adhyāsa* operates, with *turīya* as the always-already-real consciousness that is mistakenly superimposed upon by the other three. The work is one of the school's principal Upaniṣadic exegeses and shows that the Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā can be carried through on a textually contested base.

engaged_works.title: Salient Features of Shankara Vedanta
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored English summary intended for the modern reader.
engaged_works.summary: An English exposition summarising the Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā school's position for an audience without Sanskrit pāṭhaśālā training. The work compresses the doctrinal substance of the *Mūlāvidyā-Nirāsa*, the *Vedānta-Prakriyā-Pratyabhijñā*, and the *Śuddha-Śāṅkara-Prakriyā-Bhāskara* into a readable presentation, and supplies the school's principal entry-point for English-language readers. Its function is missionary-pedagogical rather than philosophical-frontier; the technical work is housed in the Sanskrit treatises. The treatise covers the school's central contentions — *adhyāsa* against *mūlāvidyā*, the *mahā-vākyas* as direct *jñāna*-generating instruments, the *adhyāsa-bhāṣya* as the methodological key to Śaṅkara's whole *bhāṣya*-corpus — in a register accessible to readers of modern Vedānta literature, and is the principal vehicle through which the Holenarsipur position has reached anglophone scholarship.
